preamble: The transformation of a voiced aspirated stop into a voiceless fricative is a core component of Grimm's Law for PIE *bh. In Germanic, PIE voiced aspirates became voiceless fricatives word-initially, exemplified by *bh > f.

examples.note: PIE voiced aspirate *bh shifted to f in word-initial position.
PIE *bh shifted to f in word-initial position.

sources: Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction. Wiley-Blackwell.
Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
